lessons.title: The First Word
lessons.gloss: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
lessons.focus: Introduce the first four letters: א, ב, ג, ד. The child sees them in context of בְּרֵאשִׁית and בָּרָא before learning their names. Hebrew reads right to left: this is the primary mechanical lesson.
lessons.vocab: בְּרֵאשִׁית (beginning), בָּרָא (he created). The child learns these two words whole, as sight-chunks, before they can parse them.
lessons.games: Chant Along (hear Genesis 1:1 three times with cantillation), Letter Tap (tap א, ב, ג, ד as they appear), Scroll Reader stage 1 (trace each letter's direction of writing).
lessons.exit: Child can name and draw א, ב, ג, ד and recite Genesis 1:1 along with the audio.

lessons.title: Letters Five to Eight
lessons.gloss: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
lessons.focus: Introduce ה, ו, ז, ח. The child notices ה appearing in הַשָּׁמַיִם and הָאָרֶץ and begins to sense that this letter has a job (definite article, not yet named).
lessons.vocab: הַשָּׁמַיִם (the heavens), הָאָרֶץ (the earth). Still taught as whole chunks.
lessons.games: Chant Along, Letter Tap across all eight letters learned so far, Picture Match (tap the picture of sky when the audio says הַשָּׁמַיִם).
lessons.exit: Child can distinguish ה from ח reliably and can point to הַשָּׁמַיִם and הָאָרֶץ in written Genesis 1:1.

lessons.title: Letters Nine to Twelve and the First Vowel
lessons.gloss: And the earth was chaos and void.
lessons.focus: Introduce ט, י, כ, ל. Introduce the first vowel point: qamatz (the long "a" sound), using הָאָרֶץ as the anchor word. The child hears the qamatz under letters they already know.
lessons.games: Chant Along, Vowel Tap (tap every qamatz you see on the screen), Verse Builder stage 1 (assemble "ha-aretz" from letter tiles).
lessons.exit: Child recognizes qamatz and can read הָאָרֶץ aloud unassisted.

lessons.title: Letters Thirteen to Sixteen and the Second Vowel
lessons.gloss: And darkness was over the face of the deep.
lessons.focus: Introduce מ (and its final form ם), נ (and ן), ס, ע. Introduce holam (the long "o" sound). The child sees חֹשֶׁךְ and recognizes the ch sound they heard in the audio.
lessons.roots: None yet. Letters and vowels only.
lessons.games: Chant Along, Letter Tap (final forms), Vowel Tap (qamatz and holam), Verse Builder (assemble חֹשֶׁךְ).
lessons.exit: Child can identify final mem and final nun and can read חֹשֶׁךְ aloud.

lessons.title: Letters Seventeen to Twenty and the Third Vowel
lessons.gloss: And God said, Let there be light. And there was light.
lessons.focus: Introduce פ (and ף), צ (and ץ), ק, ר. Introduce tzere (the "ei" sound) through the word בְּרֵאשִׁית which the child has been chanting for four days. The meeting of letter and name finally clicks.
lessons.roots: א־מ־ר (to say), first root introduced. The child meets it in וַיֹּאמֶר and is told explicitly: this word family means saying.
lessons.vocab: אוֹר (light), יְהִי (let there be), וַיֹּאמֶר (and he said).
lessons.games: Chant Along, Shoresh Sort stage 1 (two words share the root א־מ־ר, one does not, tap the outsider), Vowel Tap, Picture Match (tap the picture of light).
lessons.exit: Child recognizes the root א־מ־ר when it appears in two different forms and can read אוֹר aloud.

lessons.title: The Last Letters and a Fourth Vowel
lessons.gloss: And God saw the light, that it was good, and God separated between the light and the darkness.
lessons.focus: Introduce ש (both shin and sin), ת. Twenty-two letters complete. Introduce chirik (the "i" sound) through כִּי. Introduce the word טוֹב as a reading reward.
lessons.roots: ר־א־ה (to see). The child meets it in וַיַּרְא.
lessons.games: Chant Along, full Letter Tap across all 22 letters, Shoresh Sort, Vowel Tap, Verse Builder (assemble כִּי־טוֹב).
lessons.exit: Child can read and name every letter of the aleph-bet and can read טוֹב and כִּי aloud.

lessons.title: The Rest of the Vowels
lessons.gloss: And God called the light day, and the darkness he called night. And there was evening and there was morning, day one.
lessons.focus: Introduce patach, segol, shuruk, kubutz, shva. All vowels now in play. The child sees the full vocalization system applied to a verse they have heard for seven days.
lessons.roots: ק־ר־א (to call, to read). Introduced in וַיִּקְרָא and קָרָא.
lessons.games: Chant Along, Vowel Tap (all vowels), Shoresh Sort (three members of ק־ר־א family), Picture Match (day, night, evening, morning).
lessons.exit: Child can read יוֹם, לַיְלָה, עֶרֶב, בֹקֶר, אֶחָד aloud and identify all standard vowel points.

lessons.title: The Aleph-Bet Song and First Reading
lessons.gloss: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth, plus verses 2 through 5.
lessons.focus: Teach the aleph-bet in its traditional order with a sung melody (an original melody, not copyrighted). Then: the child reads Genesis 1:1 through 1:5 aloud in full with audio support. This is the unit exit.
lessons.games: Aleph-Bet Song (sing-along), Chant Along, Scroll Reader (the child reads verses 1 to 5 aloud, recording optional), Verse Builder (assemble verse 1 from scrambled words).
lessons.exit: Child records themselves reading Genesis 1:1 through 1:5 aloud. Parent or teacher reviews. Unit 1 complete.
